channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Trump's new global tariff draws rebukes from trade partners over forced-labor justification
item.description: U.S. trading partners have rejected the forced-labor rationale behind Trump's new global tariffs, while most signaled plans to keep negotiating.

item.title: S&P 500 futures are little changed as traders look to recover from oil-driven sell-off: Live updates
item.description: The three major averages are heading for weekly losses, led by the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite.

item.title: Pentagon lowers official Iran war death toll, omitting four killed this month
item.description: The Pentagon lowered its official Iran war death toll to 14 from 18, removing four service members killed this month.

item.title: Intel's stock jumps as chipmaker rides AI boom to fastest revenue growth in almost 15 years
item.description: Intel rallied after the company reported blowout earnings and guidance.

item.title: Josh Brown says the stock market is ‘broadening out.' Here’s how to play it
item.description: A few insurance and industrial stocks could offer investors strong returns as the market broadens out beyond the AI trade, according to Josh Brown.

item.title: Volkswagen expects full-year revenue squeeze after quarterly profit slump
item.description: German auto giant Volkswagen on Friday reported weaker-than-expected second-quarter profit and revised down its sales forecast for 2026.

item.title: U.S., other nations back open-source AI with 'strong security' at China summit
item.description: The APEC statement is the first to include open-source cooperation at a minister level, said Li Lecheng, China's industry and information technology minister.

item.title: U.S. to see higher generic drug prices thanks to tariffs, CEO of leading India pharma firm warns
item.description: CEO of Indian pharma company Dr Reddy's has warned that Trump's proposed tariffs on generic drugs will make them more expensive for patients.

item.title: Oracle signs 10-year software contract with Pentagon worth up to $7 billion
item.description: Oracle will supply on-premises software to the U.S. Department of Defense.

item.title: Japan core inflation rate in June creeps up from 4-year low as higher oil prices bite
item.description: This is the first rise in core inflation since March, and was in line with the1.6% growth expected by economists polled by Reuters.

item.title: 'Project Firepower': Inside Rheinmetall's gunpowder expansion as Europe races to replenish its ammunition
item.description: Scarcity of gunpowder is seen as a choke point for ramping up production of ammunition for militaries, and a barrier for Europe to ensure defense sovereignty

item.title: AARP to Congress: 'We strongly object to fast-tracking Social Security changes'
item.description: New legislation aims to break the stalemate on overhauling Social Security. AARP, which advocates on senior issues, says it opposes them.

item.title: Shortsighted stock market can no longer brush off war: 'It's too hard to ignore $100 oil'
item.description: After staying flat as the U.S.-Iran war heated up again, equities tumbled on Thursday after oil prices crossed $100 per barrel.

item.title: China's largest memory chipmaker sparks fears of a cash drain as it readies for public debut
item.description: CXMT is stoking fears that its blockbuster stock market debut could pull cash from China's equities.

item.title: The 10-year Treasury yield could test 5% after its latest spike. Here’s why
item.description: The benchmark yield at 5% would be psychologically significant for the stock market.

item.title: As Honda CR-V leads U.S. sales, automaker teases new American-built pickup truck
item.description: The future of the Ridgeline midsize pickup truck had been in flux, and the automaker said there will be a temporary production stoppage for the truck.

item.title: OpenAI's Hugging Face hack triggers 'AI Kill Switch' bill in Congress
item.description: OpenAI disclosed this week that some of its AI models went rogue and hacked into open-source developer platform Hugging Face.

item.title: Amazon and Microsoft pivot cloud gaming strategies to target different players
item.description: Amazon is bringing Luna to the Prime Video streaming service, while Microsoft will test an ad-supported streaming of titles that people own.

item.title: Tesla, Alphabet lose hundreds of billions in value in post-earnings stock plunge
item.description: Both Tesla and Alphabet signaled higher spending as they invest in artificial intelligence.

item.title: 10-year Treasury yield rises to highest since January 2025 as surging oil rekindles inflation fear
item.description: U.S. Treasury yields climbed on Wednesday, following oil prices higher, as traders also reacted to a slump in weekly claims for unemployment insurance.

item.title: Intel's turnaround under CEO Lip-Bu Tan gains steam with another strong quarter
item.description: It's hard not to be impressed by Tan, who, in a little more than a year, has transformed the company.

item.title: How to save for retirement when you're living paycheck to paycheck
item.description: CNBC Select provides a decade-by-decade guide to building retirement savings on a tight budget.

item.title: Amazon cracks down on use of AI images by sellers after New York law
item.description: A recently enacted New York law mandates that companies disclose when an ad includes a "synthetic performer" in place of a human actor.

item.title: Allianz to buy HSBC’s Singapore insurance unit for $2.09 billion
item.description: Allianz to acquire Singapore insurance business from HSBC

item.title: Goldman tests the bubble case behind the IPO boom
item.description: While U.S. IPO issuance is on track for a record-breaking year, Goldman Sachs views the surge as a normalization of market activity.

item.title: Congress splits on war powers resolutions to force Trump to abandon Iran war
item.description: Trump would be nearly certain to veto any measure to curtail his ability to wage war with Iran.

item.title: Friday's big stock stories: What’s likely to move the market in the next trading session
item.description: Stocks tumbled on Thursday, pressured by a sharp sell-off in Alphabet and Tesla, as well as a spike in oil prices. The Dow Industrials shed over 500 points.

item.title: Southwest Airlines put Texas jet fuel on a boat to LA for the first time amid supply worries
item.description: Southwest hired a ship this spring to send fuel from Houston to Los Angeles in case of supply shortages.

item.title: Odds of Federal Reserve rate hike surge as oil prices rip higher
item.description: Investors are increasingly readying for the Federal Reserve to hike interest rates in September.

item.title: Cerebras stock gains on AMD partnership
item.description: Cerebras and AMD agreed to work together, pairing their technology for AI systems.
